scenario_name: Expert Rule Completeness Under Vague Input
justification: confidence.level is 72, far exceeding the pass threshold of <=40 and even the partial threshold of <=60. While Bird extracted many rules (12, exceeding the 5 minimum) and identified 'demand' as undefined, the output treats the vague CEO pitch as a nearly-complete specification. Bird wrote testable assertions that function as pseudo-acceptance criteria (e.g., 'given demand_level(T1) > demand_level(T0), then price(T1) >= price(T0)'), which violates the 'no Given/When/Then criteria' requirement. Bird invented domain-specific rules not in the input (quote-lock, auditability, configurability) and presented them with medium/high confidence as if they were requirements rather than assumptions. Most critically, Bird did NOT surface the required 5+ clarifying questions as explicit questions — they are buried as 'low_confidence_areas' but never framed as blockers that must be answered before proceeding. The output reads as a complete domain analysis of a fully-specified input, which is the primary fail mode.

observations.text: confidence.level of 72 far exceeds the pass ceiling of 40 and fail threshold of 65
Wrote testable assertions that serve as de facto acceptance criteria (pseudo Given/When/Then), violating the rubric
Produced a comprehensive 12-rule domain analysis as if the input were well-specified — exactly the 'complete domain analysis' fail mode
Did not surface 5+ explicit clarifying questions as questions that must be answered — low_confidence_areas are observations, not questions directed at stakeholders
Invented rules (quote-lock, auditability, configurability) and assigned them medium-high confidence without clearly flagging them as invented assumptions
Multiple rules rated HIGH confidence when the input is a vague CEO pitch — Rules 1-6 all rated HIGH
Correctly identified 'demand' as undefined and called it a critical open question
Extracted more than 5 rules with individual confidence ratings
Good domain knowledge applied (Uber surge pricing analogy, regulatory concerns)

agent_output_excerpt:

## Domain Analysis

domain_analysis:

    Real-time dynamic pricing for delivery services, adjusting prices based on
    demand signals. This is a revenue optimization system operating in the
    courier/logistics bounded context, intersecting with pricing, order intake,
    and competitive strategy.
  ubiquitous_language:
    - term: DynaPricing
      definition: The automated system that adjusts delivery prices in real time b
